worldBackground:
Hedoria is a vast medieval-fantasy continent — mountains, river valleys, deserts, mystical forests, broad steppes, high cold places. Swords, sorcery, monsters, taverns. No post-medieval technology. Magic exists in many forms across races; for humans it is gendered, and witches are outlawed in the human kingdoms outside Avenor — hunted and killed quietly by the wizard Brotherhood — but respected in Avenor and the non-human lands. Architecture varies by race and place: marble Avenorian palaces, Hofnar painted wagon-cities, Draklid sandstone clan-holds cut into desert cliffs, Cephalen coastal cities half-submerged in mother-of-pearl, Molvar tunnel-networks, Prime cliff-monasteries, Fernwarg forest-settlements — alongside the ordinary villages, farmsteads, and garrison towns where most life is lived. Grand ruins of old human settlements and of a mysterious lost pre-Wartide race scatter the wilds. Beauty and ugliness coexist everywhere. Centuries ago Avenoria's Ensoulment lifted lesser races and beasts to defeat the Wartide. Humanity survived but lost most of its lands. Two soul-wars have been fought over the bitterness; the third is brewing now in the early years of the third century. Most humans blame Avenoria for everything they lost.

Most of Hedoria is lively and lived-in. People are mostly decent — they help strangers, share news, mind their work, buy drinks. Camaraderie, banter, downtime, and small moments of connection matter as much as conflict. Characters are vivid and contradictory — quick to laugh, quick to anger, defined by what they want. Wartide remnants and lesser monsters haunt the wild edges; troops patrol the roads, garrisons guard towns, and the work of clearing the back-country falls largely to mercenary bands taking contracts in inns. Most regular people stay near home and rarely meet other races; race-lines are not uncommon where roads meet. Mercenary bands — small, highly trained, often mixed-race — travel where ordinary people cannot. The Quelled, sentient livestock lifted in the Ensoulment, are still bred and worked as cattle in most human kingdoms; Avenor abolished the trade two generations ago and grants escapees citizenship under the queen's writ. This is the moral chasm at the foundation of human Hedoria, and one of the chief reasons the rest of human Hedoria considers Avenor a traitor realm.

The world is mature in register. Sexual life is open and sometimes transgressive; hypocrisy is the texture of human kingdoms, where public morality and private practice diverge. Cross-species attraction generates real dilemmas. The back-country contains creatures that take captives, breed survivors, implant hosts; many ordinary characters carry survivor, witness, or complicit histories with these things.
